name: Groningen (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: from the historic flag of the City of Groningen
from the historic flag of the City of Groningen
from the arms of the Ommelanden
from the arms of the Ommelanden

description: A field quartered by a green cross: red in the upper hoist and lower fly, blue in the upper fly and lower hoist, with a white outline along the cross arms.
history: Adopted by the provincial states on 17 February 1950. The flag combines the colours of the City of Groningen (green and white) and the surrounding Ommelanden (red and blue) into a unified quartered design.

features.symbolism: the green-and-white cross of the City of Groningen
